intro: Dolichorrhiza correvoniana, also known as the 'Tibetan Rhubarb', is a perennial plant species native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas and Tibet. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are beneficial for treating various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Dolichorrhiza correvoniana include glycosides, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
